title: cereaster flagelliformis
intro: Cereaster flagelliformis, also known as the "Snake Flower" or "Cobra Plant", is a type of flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, long-tubed flowers that resemble snakes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Cereaster flagelliformis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are said to have various health benefits when consumed.
